image_path.description: Path relative to <task_workspace>/files/output/, e.g. 'charts/T3_forecast.png'. Absolute paths outside the task workspace are rejected.

subagent.name: feishu_upload_image

subagent.description: List every file currently in this task's workspace, including files the user uploaded (`uploaded`), inputs received from previous tool calls (`input`), and previous-turn deliverables you wrote with write_file (`output`). Call this at the start of a turn whenever the user references 'the file we made', '上一轮的产物', '刚才那份报告', or any prior artifact you may have lost from context. Returns id/name/scope/format/size.
subagent.name: list_files

subagent.description: Read the contents of a file in this task's workspace. Accepts either the file's `id` (preferred — get from list_files) or its `name`. Text formats (.md/.txt/.csv/.tsv/.sql/.py/.json/.log/.yml/.yaml) return UTF-8 content; binary files return {is_binary: true} so you can advise the user to open it. Use this to pick up where a previous turn left off — e.g. read `query.sql` from last round before refining it.
subagent.name: read_file

subagent.description: Read a file from the current Agent's knowledge base (agents/<agent_id>/knowledge/<path>). Use this to fetch SQL templates, event-tracking indexes, page-structure specs, historical cases, and other reference data that is NOT part of the always-on system prompt. Start by reading `index.yaml` to see what's available, then drill in. Supports text formats (.yaml/.yml/.md/.json/.txt/.sql). Binary files (.db, images) are rejected — ask the user or use a different tool. Path must be relative within the agent's knowledge directory; '..' and absolute paths are rejected.

path.description: Relative path inside knowledge/, e.g. 'index.yaml', 'metrics/sql_templates/browser_feed.yaml', 'analysis/cases_and_lessons.yaml'.

subagent.name: read_agent_knowledge

subagent.description: Fetch the skill's instruction file. This task has these agentic skills bound: `kyuubi`, `feishu`, `nl-python`. Calling read_skill on any OTHER skill id returns SKILL_NOT_FOUND — only the listed ids are available. Default (no `path`) = returns SKILL.md. To read a sibling reference file that the SKILL.md points you to (e.g. `reference/browser/table-schema.md`), pass `path` with the relative path. Pass `path="/"` or `path="ls"` to list all files in the skill folder. Content is read from the task's own snapshot, so answers are reproducible even if the global skill is updated later.

path.description: Optional. Relative path inside the skill dir (e.g. 'reference/browser/table-schema.md'). 'ls' or '/' lists all files.

subagent.name: read_skill

subagent.description: Maintain a live TODO list for the current task so the user can see your progress. Use this when the task has 3+ distinct steps. Call with the FULL list every time (replace-all semantics, not diff-merge). Before you start working on an item, mark it in_progress. IMMEDIATELY after finishing, mark it completed — do NOT batch completions. At most ONE item may be in_progress at a time. If blocked, keep the item in_progress and add a new pending item describing the blocker.

activeForm.description: Present continuous form, e.g. 'Writing funnel SQL'

subagent.name: todo_write

subagent.description: Pause the current task and ask the user to provide a concrete decision, correction, approval, or missing business context. Use this only when continuing without a human answer would be unsafe, ambiguous, or likely to produce incorrect results. After calling this tool, stop and wait for the user's response.

message.description: What you need from the user and why.

resume_prompt.description: Instruction to follow after the user resolves the request.

table.description: Optional rows needing review, e.g. {columns:[...], rows:[{...}]}

subagent.description: Execute Python code in a sandboxed subprocess. Two main uses: (1) data analysis with pandas / numpy / scipy / sklearn / statsmodels / prophet / ruptures / matplotlib / seaborn pre-installed; (2) driving CLI tools that an agentic skill (feishu / kyuubi / datum / etc.) tells you to run — call them with subprocess.run([...], capture_output=True, text=True). Network is ON and the host's CLI auth files are reachable (HOME inherited), so `feishu fetch`, `kyuubi sql query`, `datum query` etc. work. Wall-clock + CPU timeout 60s, memory 1GB, output files captured under <task_workspace>/files/output/. cwd is files/output/. Print key results to stdout for the user; write artifacts to disk.

image_path.description: Path relative to <task_workspace>/files/output/, e.g. 'charts/T3_forecast.png'. Absolute paths outside the task workspace are rejected.

no_whitelist.name: feishu_upload_image

no_whitelist.description: List every file currently in this task's workspace, including files the user uploaded (`uploaded`), inputs received from previous tool calls (`input`), and previous-turn deliverables you wrote with write_file (`output`). Call this at the start of a turn whenever the user references 'the file we made', '上一轮的产物', '刚才那份报告', or any prior artifact you may have lost from context. Returns id/name/scope/format/size.
no_whitelist.name: list_files

no_whitelist.description: Read the contents of a file in this task's workspace. Accepts either the file's `id` (preferred — get from list_files) or its `name`. Text formats (.md/.txt/.csv/.tsv/.sql/.py/.json/.log/.yml/.yaml) return UTF-8 content; binary files return {is_binary: true} so you can advise the user to open it. Use this to pick up where a previous turn left off — e.g. read `query.sql` from last round before refining it.
no_whitelist.name: read_file

no_whitelist.description: Send a Feishu bot message, not a document. Prefer custom bot webhook delivery for 日报推送 / 发群: pass webhook_url and optional sign_secret. If webhook_url is omitted, this falls back to app IM delivery with FEISHU_APP_ID + FEISHU_APP_SECRET and receive_id. For the 日报推送 agent, webhook_url is required. Return the Feishu API result; include success/failure in reply.

content.description: Message body in Feishu lark_md markdown. Keep it concise when image_path is provided.

html_url.description: Optional absolute URL to the HTML report. When provided, the card adds a visible link to open the full HTML report.

image_path.description: Optional PNG/JPG path relative to <task_workspace>/files/output/, e.g. 'daily_report_preview.png'. When provided, the image is uploaded as a Feishu message image and embedded in the bot card.

receive_id.description: App IM target id. Optional if FEISHU_DEFAULT_RECEIVE_ID is configured. Ignored when webhook_url is provided.

receive_id_type.description: App IM target id type. Defaults to FEISHU_DEFAULT_RECEIVE_ID_TYPE or chat_id. Ignored when webhook_url is provided.

sign_secret.description: Optional Feishu custom bot signing secret. Do not echo it back.

template.description: Feishu card header color template. Defaults to blue.

title.description: Card title, e.g. 内容生态数据日报 - 2026-06-03.

webhook_url.description: Feishu custom bot webhook URL, e.g. https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/bot/v2/hook/...

no_whitelist.name: feishu_send_message

no_whitelist.description: Publish a markdown document to Feishu (lark) via the bundled `feishu` CLI. By default lands in the team wiki space configured in FEISHU_DEFAULT_WIKI_SPACE_ID — every space member already has read access (no manual permission request needed). After creation, the task owner + active collaborators (those with a xiaomi_email on file) are auto-granted the FEISHU_AUTO_PERM_LEVEL permission; passing extra `share_to` emails grants them too. Returns the doc URL on success — INCLUDE the URL in your reply. 图表必须用 PNG（execute_python 出图 → feishu_upload_image 嵌入），不要在 markdown 里写 ```mermaid``` 块——飞书 PlantUML 渲染未开通，mermaid 会变成空白画板。返回值若带 content_warnings/hint 字段，说明文档里有渲染失败的块，按 hint 指引重试。

folder.description: Optional: put the doc under this drive folder token. Mutually exclusive with wiki_space / wiki_node.

share_perm.description: Permission level for share_to addresses. Defaults to 'edit'. Task owner + collaborators always get FEISHU_AUTO_PERM_LEVEL regardless.

share_to.description: Optional Xiaomi office emails (xxx@xiaomi.com / @mi.com) to grant explicit perms in addition to the task owner + collaborators. Use when sharing with a stakeholder who is not yet on the task.

wiki_node.description: Optional: put the doc under this wiki node (token or URL). Mutually exclusive with wiki_space / folder.

wiki_space.description: Optional override: place the doc under this wiki space root. Defaults to FEISHU_DEFAULT_WIKI_SPACE_ID.

no_whitelist.name: feishu_publish

no_whitelist.description: Read a file from the current Agent's knowledge base (agents/<agent_id>/knowledge/<path>). Use this to fetch SQL templates, event-tracking indexes, page-structure specs, historical cases, and other reference data that is NOT part of the always-on system prompt. Start by reading `index.yaml` to see what's available, then drill in. Supports text formats (.yaml/.yml/.md/.json/.txt/.sql). Binary files (.db, images) are rejected — ask the user or use a different tool. Path must be relative within the agent's knowledge directory; '..' and absolute paths are rejected.

path.description: Relative path inside knowledge/, e.g. 'index.yaml', 'metrics/sql_templates/browser_feed.yaml', 'analysis/cases_and_lessons.yaml'.

no_whitelist.name: read_agent_knowledge

no_whitelist.description: Fetch the skill's instruction file. This task has these agentic skills bound: `feishu`. Calling read_skill on any OTHER skill id returns SKILL_NOT_FOUND — only the listed ids are available. Default (no `path`) = returns SKILL.md. To read a sibling reference file that the SKILL.md points you to (e.g. `reference/browser/table-schema.md`), pass `path` with the relative path. Pass `path="/"` or `path="ls"` to list all files in the skill folder. Content is read from the task's own snapshot, so answers are reproducible even if the global skill is updated later.

path.description: Optional. Relative path inside the skill dir (e.g. 'reference/browser/table-schema.md'). 'ls' or '/' lists all files.

no_whitelist.name: read_skill

no_whitelist.description: Persist a reusable memory for the current user or for the current user+agent. Use only for stable preferences, feedback, project context, or external reference pointers that should appear in future conversations. Do not save secrets, one-off facts, or rules already fixed in the system prompt.
no_whitelist.name: memory_save

no_whitelist.description: Delete a stale or incorrect memory and remove it from MEMORY.md.
no_whitelist.name: memory_delete

no_whitelist.description: Overwrite the current task's STATE.md with a concise markdown summary of semantic progress: phase, decomposition, pending items, key decisions, current files, and next steps. Keep it under about 100 lines.
no_whitelist.name: task_state_save

no_whitelist.description: Maintain a live TODO list for the current task so the user can see your progress. Use this when the task has 3+ distinct steps. Call with the FULL list every time (replace-all semantics, not diff-merge). Before you start working on an item, mark it in_progress. IMMEDIATELY after finishing, mark it completed — do NOT batch completions. At most ONE item may be in_progress at a time. If blocked, keep the item in_progress and add a new pending item describing the blocker.

activeForm.description: Present continuous form, e.g. 'Writing funnel SQL'

no_whitelist.name: todo_write

no_whitelist.description: Pause the current task and ask the user to provide a concrete decision, correction, approval, or missing business context. Use this only when continuing without a human answer would be unsafe, ambiguous, or likely to produce incorrect results. After calling this tool, stop and wait for the user's response.

fields.description: Optional input fields for the UI.

message.description: What you need from the user and why.

resume_prompt.description: Instruction to follow after the user resolves the request.

table.description: Optional rows needing review, e.g. {columns:[...], rows:[{...}]}

no_whitelist.name: request_human_input

no_whitelist.description: Submit the completed plan to the user for approval and STOP generating. Call this ONLY when you have finished planning and have a concrete, actionable plan. After you call this tool, do NOT continue generating text — the user will review, and when they approve, you will be re-invoked with the green-light to execute. The plan argument should be a complete markdown description covering: what will be changed, in which files, and what verification to run.
no_whitelist.name: exit_plan_mode

no_whitelist.description: Delegate a bounded sub-task to a specialized agent. The sub-agent runs with its own system prompt, tool budget, and isolated conversation, and returns only its final text answer (not its full transcript) — protecting your context. Any files the sub-agent writes (write_file) land in THIS task's workspace, so the user sees them. Use this to offload research (e.g. 'spawn data-cleaner to clean users.csv'), long analyses, or when a different persona is better suited. Cannot be nested (sub-agents cannot spawn sub-agents).

agent_id.description: The agent to delegate to. Must be one of the listed existing spawn targets.

allowed_tools.description: Optional whitelist of tool names the sub-agent may use.

max_tokens.description: Max tokens per LLM call inside the sub-agent.

prompt.description: The complete task description for the sub-agent.

no_whitelist.name: spawn_subagent

no_whitelist.description: Create a persistent cron schedule for the current task. Use only after the user has confirmed the schedule, cadence, and delivery target. The schedule will appear in the task's Scheduled tab and in the global scheduled task dashboard.

cron.description: Standard 5-field cron expression: minute hour day month weekday.

enabled.description: Whether the schedule is enabled immediately. Defaults to true.

model.description: Optional model id for scheduled execution.

prompt.description: Full instruction prompt to execute every time the cron fires.

todo_list.description: Optional short execution checklist for the scheduled run.

no_whitelist.name: create_scheduled_task

no_whitelist.description: Enqueue a long-running task to execute in the background. Returns immediately with a background job id. When the job completes, a notification is pushed to the user and any files the background agent produces land in this task's workspace. Use for tasks estimated to take >2 minutes or that the user does not need to watch live.

title.description: Short human-readable label shown in notifications.

no_whitelist.name: run_background

no_whitelist.description: Call a data-platform-mcp tool. The data platform provides 35 tools across 8 modules: consistency (metric consistency), metric, dimension, dataset, table, metadata, domain, version. Pass the MCP tool name and its arguments as a JSON object. The server proxies the call to https://data-platform-mcp.mib.miui.com/mcp and returns the result as structured text. Use this whenever the user asks about 指标 / 维度 / 数据集 / 数据表 / 业务域 / 元数据 / 版本管理 / 指标一致性检测.

arguments.description: Arguments for the MCP tool. Each tool has its own schema; consult the skill docs for details.

tool_name.description: MCP tool name, e.g. 'metadata_config_list', 'metric_list', 'domain_tree', 'consistency_overview'. See the data-platform-mcp skill for the full list of 35 tools.

no_whitelist.name: data_platform_call

subagent.description: Return the current UTC datetime in ISO 8601.
subagent.name: now

subagent.description: Write text content to a file in the current task workspace. The file is registered under tasks/{task_id}/files/output/ and appears immediately in the user's left-side file panel. subagent.name: write_file

subagent.description: Run the volcano ABtest analysis pipeline for ONE experiment and return a markdown report. Wraps agents/volcano-abtest/scripts/analyze.py via the host's `datum` CLI — queries doris_zjyprc_hadoop ABtest tables (weighted aggregates + p-values + daily trends) and formats a report. Use this whenever the user asks for 火山实验 / abtest analysis with an experiment id + date range. Report is also saved to the task workspace as `abtest_<media>_<exp_id>_<start>-<end>.md` so it shows up in the user's file panel. Return field `report_md` is the same markdown — paste it into your reply, then append your own ### 实验分析 section.

end_date.description: End date. Same formats as start_date.

media.description: Media type. One of: 浏览器 / browser (浏览器), 内容中心 / 桌面内容中心 / newhome / nh / mcc (桌面内容中心).

default.name: now

default.description: Echo back the given text. Useful for testing tool calling.

default.description: Run a SELECT against Xiaomi Kyuubi SQL gateway. Read-only. The server already has the connection context configured (region=chnbj, workspace=11329, catalog=iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop, engine=presto, token=***). Do NOT ask the user for any of these — just call with the `sql` argument. Use fully-qualified table names like `iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop.<schema>.<table>`. Always include LIMIT.

sql.description: The SELECT statement to execute. Must include LIMIT.

default.name: kyuubi_query

default.description: Write text content to a file in the current task workspace. The file is registered under tasks/{task_id}/files/output/ and appears immediately in the user's left-side file panel. Use this whenever you produce a deliverable the user should be able to open / download / iterate on (markdown report, SQL script, CSV data, JSON, etc.). DO NOT use it for in-line answers.
default.name: write_file

default.description: Execute Python code in a sandboxed subprocess. Two main uses: (1) data analysis with pandas / numpy / scipy / sklearn / statsmodels / prophet / ruptures / matplotlib / seaborn pre-installed; (2) driving CLI tools that an agentic skill (feishu / kyuubi / datum / etc.) tells you to run — call them with subprocess.run([...], capture_output=True, text=True). Network is ON and the host's CLI auth files are reachable (HOME inherited), so `feishu fetch`, `kyuubi sql query`, `datum query` etc. work. Wall-clock + CPU timeout 60s, memory 1GB, output files captured under <task_workspace>/files/output/. cwd is files/output/. Print key results to stdout for the user; write artifacts to disk.

code.description: Full Python source. Use relative paths (cwd = <task_workspace>/files/output/). Print key results to stdout for the LLM/user to see; write artifacts to disk.

description.description: One-line summary of what this run computes (audit / display, not behavior).

timeout_sec.description: Optional override for wall-clock + CPU limit; defaults to ICE_PYTHON_SANDBOX_TIMEOUT_SEC.

default.name: execute_python

fields.description: Optional input fields for the UI.

message.description: What you need from the user and why.

resume_prompt.description: Instruction to follow after the user resolves the request.

table.description: Optional rows needing review, e.g. {columns:[...], rows:[{...}]}

default.name: request_human_input

default.description: Submit the completed plan to the user for approval and STOP generating. Call this ONLY when you have finished planning and have a concrete, actionable plan. After you call this tool, do NOT continue generating text — the user will review, and when they approve, you will be re-invoked with the green-light to execute. The plan argument should be a complete markdown description covering: what will be changed, in which files, and what verification to run.
default.name: exit_plan_mode

default.description: Delegate a bounded sub-task to a specialized agent. The sub-agent runs with its own system prompt, tool budget, and isolated conversation, and returns only its final text answer (not its full transcript) — protecting your context. Any files the sub-agent writes (write_file) land in THIS task's workspace, so the user sees them. Use this to offload research (e.g. 'spawn data-cleaner to clean users.csv'), long analyses, or when a different persona is better suited. Cannot be nested (sub-agents cannot spawn sub-agents).

agent_id.description: The agent to delegate to. Must be one of the listed existing spawn targets.

allowed_tools.description: Optional whitelist of tool names the sub-agent may use.

max_tokens.description: Max tokens per LLM call inside the sub-agent.

prompt.description: The complete task description for the sub-agent.

default.name: spawn_subagent

default.description: Create a persistent cron schedule for the current task. Use only after the user has confirmed the schedule, cadence, and delivery target. The schedule will appear in the task's Scheduled tab and in the global scheduled task dashboard.

cron.description: Standard 5-field cron expression: minute hour day month weekday.

enabled.description: Whether the schedule is enabled immediately. Defaults to true.

model.description: Optional model id for scheduled execution.

prompt.description: Full instruction prompt to execute every time the cron fires.

todo_list.description: Optional short execution checklist for the scheduled run.

default.name: create_scheduled_task

default.description: Enqueue a long-running task to execute in the background. Returns immediately with a background job id. When the job completes, a notification is pushed to the user and any files the background agent produces land in this task's workspace. Use for tasks estimated to take >2 minutes or that the user does not need to watch live.

title.description: Short human-readable label shown in notifications.

default.name: run_background

default.description: Call a data-platform-mcp tool. The data platform provides 35 tools across 8 modules: consistency (metric consistency), metric, dimension, dataset, table, metadata, domain, version. Pass the MCP tool name and its arguments as a JSON object. The server proxies the call to https://data-platform-mcp.mib.miui.com/mcp and returns the result as structured text. Use this whenever the user asks about 指标 / 维度 / 数据集 / 数据表 / 业务域 / 元数据 / 版本管理 / 指标一致性检测.

arguments.description: Arguments for the MCP tool. Each tool has its own schema; consult the skill docs for details.

tool_name.description: MCP tool name, e.g. 'metadata_config_list', 'metric_list', 'domain_tree', 'consistency_overview'. See the data-platform-mcp skill for the full list of 35 tools.

default.name: data_platform_call

no_skills.description: Return the current UTC datetime in ISO 8601.
no_skills.name: now

no_skills.description: Echo back the given text. Useful for testing tool calling.
no_skills.name: echo

no_skills.description: Run a SELECT against Xiaomi Kyuubi SQL gateway. Read-only. The server already has the connection context configured (region=chnbj, workspace=11329, catalog=iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop, engine=presto, token=***). Do NOT ask the user for any of these — just call with the `sql` argument. Use fully-qualified table names like `iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop.<schema>.<table>`. Always include LIMIT.

sql.description: The SELECT statement to execute. Must include LIMIT.

no_skills.name: kyuubi_query

no_skills.description: Write text content to a file in the current task workspace. The file is registered under tasks/{task_id}/files/output/ and appears immediately in the user's left-side file panel. Use this whenever you produce a deliverable the user should be able to open / download / iterate on (markdown report, SQL script, CSV data, JSON, etc.). DO NOT use it for in-line answers.
no_skills.name: write_file

no_skills.description: Execute Python code in a sandboxed subprocess. Two main uses: (1) data analysis with pandas / numpy / scipy / sklearn / statsmodels / prophet / ruptures / matplotlib / seaborn pre-installed; (2) driving CLI tools that an agentic skill (feishu / kyuubi / datum / etc.) tells you to run — call them with subprocess.run([...], capture_output=True, text=True). Network is ON and the host's CLI auth files are reachable (HOME inherited), so `feishu fetch`, `kyuubi sql query`, `datum query` etc. work. Wall-clock + CPU timeout 60s, memory 1GB, output files captured under <task_workspace>/files/output/. cwd is files/output/. Print key results to stdout for the user; write artifacts to disk.

code.description: Full Python source. Use relative paths (cwd = <task_workspace>/files/output/). Print key results to stdout for the LLM/user to see; write artifacts to disk.

description.description: One-line summary of what this run computes (audit / display, not behavior).

timeout_sec.description: Optional override for wall-clock + CPU limit; defaults to ICE_PYTHON_SANDBOX_TIMEOUT_SEC.

no_skills.name: execute_python

no_skills.description: Run the volcano ABtest analysis pipeline for ONE experiment and return a markdown report. Wraps agents/volcano-abtest/scripts/analyze.py via the host's `datum` CLI — queries doris_zjyprc_hadoop ABtest tables (weighted aggregates + p-values + daily trends) and formats a report. Use this whenever the user asks for 火山实验 / abtest analysis with an experiment id + date range. Report is also saved to the task workspace as `abtest_<media>_<exp_id>_<start>-<end>.md` so it shows up in the user's file panel. Return field `report_md` is the same markdown — paste it into your reply, then append your own ### 实验分析 section.

end_date.description: End date. Same formats as start_date.

media.description: Media type. One of: 浏览器 / browser (浏览器), 内容中心 / 桌面内容中心 / newhome / nh / mcc (桌面内容中心).

no_skills.name: volcano_abtest_analyze

no_skills.description: Upload a PNG/JPG image from the task workspace to a Feishu doc and return its image_token (so subsequent feishu docx edits can embed it). Wraps `feishu docx upload-image <doc_token> --file <path>`. Use after `feishu_publish` creates the doc and `execute_python` produces a chart PNG under <task_workspace>/files/output/charts/.

image_path.description: Path relative to <task_workspace>/files/output/, e.g. 'charts/T3_forecast.png'. Absolute paths outside the task workspace are rejected.

no_skills.name: feishu_upload_image

no_skills.description: List every file currently in this task's workspace, including files the user uploaded (`uploaded`), inputs received from previous tool calls (`input`), and previous-turn deliverables you wrote with write_file (`output`). Call this at the start of a turn whenever the user references 'the file we made', '上一轮的产物', '刚才那份报告', or any prior artifact you may have lost from context. Returns id/name/scope/format/size.
no_skills.name: list_files

no_skills.description: Read the contents of a file in this task's workspace. Accepts either the file's `id` (preferred — get from list_files) or its `name`. Text formats (.md/.txt/.csv/.tsv/.sql/.py/.json/.log/.yml/.yaml) return UTF-8 content; binary files return {is_binary: true} so you can advise the user to open it. Use this to pick up where a previous turn left off — e.g. read `query.sql` from last round before refining it.
no_skills.name: read_file

no_skills.description: Send a Feishu bot message, not a document. Prefer custom bot webhook delivery for 日报推送 / 发群: pass webhook_url and optional sign_secret. If webhook_url is omitted, this falls back to app IM delivery with FEISHU_APP_ID + FEISHU_APP_SECRET and receive_id. For the 日报推送 agent, webhook_url is required. Return the Feishu API result; include success/failure in reply.

content.description: Message body in Feishu lark_md markdown. Keep it concise when image_path is provided.

html_url.description: Optional absolute URL to the HTML report. When provided, the card adds a visible link to open the full HTML report.

image_path.description: Optional PNG/JPG path relative to <task_workspace>/files/output/, e.g. 'daily_report_preview.png'. When provided, the image is uploaded as a Feishu message image and embedded in the bot card.

receive_id.description: App IM target id. Optional if FEISHU_DEFAULT_RECEIVE_ID is configured. Ignored when webhook_url is provided.

receive_id_type.description: App IM target id type. Defaults to FEISHU_DEFAULT_RECEIVE_ID_TYPE or chat_id. Ignored when webhook_url is provided.

sign_secret.description: Optional Feishu custom bot signing secret. Do not echo it back.

template.description: Feishu card header color template. Defaults to blue.

title.description: Card title, e.g. 内容生态数据日报 - 2026-06-03.

webhook_url.description: Feishu custom bot webhook URL, e.g. https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/bot/v2/hook/...

no_skills.name: feishu_send_message

no_skills.description: Publish a markdown document to Feishu (lark) via the bundled `feishu` CLI. By default lands in the team wiki space configured in FEISHU_DEFAULT_WIKI_SPACE_ID — every space member already has read access (no manual permission request needed). After creation, the task owner + active collaborators (those with a xiaomi_email on file) are auto-granted the FEISHU_AUTO_PERM_LEVEL permission; passing extra `share_to` emails grants them too. Returns the doc URL on success — INCLUDE the URL in your reply. 图表必须用 PNG（execute_python 出图 → feishu_upload_image 嵌入），不要在 markdown 里写 ```mermaid``` 块——飞书 PlantUML 渲染未开通，mermaid 会变成空白画板。返回值若带 content_warnings/hint 字段，说明文档里有渲染失败的块，按 hint 指引重试。

folder.description: Optional: put the doc under this drive folder token. Mutually exclusive with wiki_space / wiki_node.

share_perm.description: Permission level for share_to addresses. Defaults to 'edit'. Task owner + collaborators always get FEISHU_AUTO_PERM_LEVEL regardless.

share_to.description: Optional Xiaomi office emails (xxx@xiaomi.com / @mi.com) to grant explicit perms in addition to the task owner + collaborators. Use when sharing with a stakeholder who is not yet on the task.

wiki_node.description: Optional: put the doc under this wiki node (token or URL). Mutually exclusive with wiki_space / folder.

wiki_space.description: Optional override: place the doc under this wiki space root. Defaults to FEISHU_DEFAULT_WIKI_SPACE_ID.

no_skills.name: feishu_publish

no_skills.description: Read a file from the current Agent's knowledge base (agents/<agent_id>/knowledge/<path>). Use this to fetch SQL templates, event-tracking indexes, page-structure specs, historical cases, and other reference data that is NOT part of the always-on system prompt. Start by reading `index.yaml` to see what's available, then drill in. Supports text formats (.yaml/.yml/.md/.json/.txt/.sql). Binary files (.db, images) are rejected — ask the user or use a different tool. Path must be relative within the agent's knowledge directory; '..' and absolute paths are rejected.

path.description: Relative path inside knowledge/, e.g. 'index.yaml', 'metrics/sql_templates/browser_feed.yaml', 'analysis/cases_and_lessons.yaml'.

no_skills.name: read_agent_knowledge

no_skills.description: Fetch the skill's instruction file. NOTE: this task has NO agentic skills bound — calling read_skill will return SKILL_NOT_FOUND for every skill_id. Ask the user to add a skill in the workspace 「🧰 本任务 Skills」 panel first, then retry.

path.description: Optional. Relative path inside the skill dir (e.g. 'reference/browser/table-schema.md'). 'ls' or '/' lists all files.

no_skills.name: read_skill

no_skills.description: Persist a reusable memory for the current user or for the current user+agent. Use only for stable preferences, feedback, project context, or external reference pointers that should appear in future conversations. Do not save secrets, one-off facts, or rules already fixed in the system prompt.
no_skills.name: memory_save

no_skills.description: Delete a stale or incorrect memory and remove it from MEMORY.md.
no_skills.name: memory_delete

no_skills.description: Overwrite the current task's STATE.md with a concise markdown summary of semantic progress: phase, decomposition, pending items, key decisions, current files, and next steps. Keep it under about 100 lines.
no_skills.name: task_state_save

no_skills.description: Maintain a live TODO list for the current task so the user can see your progress. Use this when the task has 3+ distinct steps. Call with the FULL list every time (replace-all semantics, not diff-merge). Before you start working on an item, mark it in_progress. IMMEDIATELY after finishing, mark it completed — do NOT batch completions. At most ONE item may be in_progress at a time. resume_prompt.description: Instruction to follow after the user resolves the request.

table.description: Optional rows needing review, e.g. {columns:[...], rows:[{...}]}

no_whitelist.name: request_human_input

no_whitelist.description: Submit the completed plan to the user for approval and STOP generating. Call this ONLY when you have finished planning and have a concrete, actionable plan. After you call this tool, do NOT continue generating text — the user will review, and when they approve, you will be re-invoked with the green-light to execute. The plan argument should be a complete markdown description covering: what will be changed, in which files, and what verification to run.
no_whitelist.name: exit_plan_mode

no_whitelist.description: Delegate a bounded sub-task to a specialized agent. The sub-agent runs with its own system prompt, tool budget, and isolated conversation, and returns only its final text answer (not its full transcript) — protecting your context. Any files the sub-agent writes (write_file) land in THIS task's workspace, so the user sees them. Use this to offload research (e.g. 'spawn data-cleaner to clean users.csv'), long analyses, or when a different persona is better suited. Cannot be nested (sub-agents cannot spawn sub-agents).

agent_id.description: The agent to delegate to. Must be one of the listed existing spawn targets.

allowed_tools.description: Optional whitelist of tool names the sub-agent may use.

max_tokens.description: Max tokens per LLM call inside the sub-agent.

prompt.description: The complete task description for the sub-agent.

no_whitelist.name: spawn_subagent

no_whitelist.description: Create a persistent cron schedule for the current task. Use only after the user has confirmed the schedule, cadence, and delivery target. The schedule will appear in the task's Scheduled tab and in the global scheduled task dashboard.

cron.description: Standard 5-field cron expression: minute hour day month weekday.

enabled.description: Whether the schedule is enabled immediately. Defaults to true.

model.description: Optional model id for scheduled execution.

prompt.description: Full instruction prompt to execute every time the cron fires.

todo_list.description: Optional short execution checklist for the scheduled run.

no_whitelist.name: create_scheduled_task

no_whitelist.description: Enqueue a long-running task to execute in the background. Returns immediately with a background job id. When the job completes, a notification is pushed to the user and any files the background agent produces land in this task's workspace. Use for tasks estimated to take >2 minutes or that the user does not need to watch live.

title.description: Short human-readable label shown in notifications.

no_whitelist.name: run_background

no_whitelist.description: Call a data-platform-mcp tool. The data platform provides 35 tools across 8 modules: consistency (metric consistency), metric, dimension, dataset, table, metadata, domain, version. Pass the MCP tool name and its arguments as a JSON object. The server proxies the call to https://data-platform-mcp.mib.miui.com/mcp and returns the result as structured text. Use this whenever the user asks about 指标 / 维度 / 数据集 / 数据表 / 业务域 / 元数据 / 版本管理 / 指标一致性检测.

arguments.description: Arguments for the MCP tool. Each tool has its own schema; consult the skill docs for details.

tool_name.description: MCP tool name, e.g. 'metadata_config_list', 'metric_list', 'domain_tree', 'consistency_overview'. See the data-platform-mcp skill for the full list of 35 tools.

no_whitelist.name: data_platform_call

plan_mode.description: Return the current UTC datetime in ISO 8601.
plan_mode.name: now

plan_mode.description: Echo back the given text. Useful for testing tool calling.
plan_mode.name: echo

plan_mode.description: Run a SELECT against Xiaomi Kyuubi SQL gateway. Read-only. The server already has the connection context configured (region=chnbj, workspace=11329, catalog=iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop, engine=presto, token=***). Do NOT ask the user for any of these — just call with the `sql` argument. Use fully-qualified table names like `iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop.<schema>.<table>`. Always include LIMIT.

sql.description: The SELECT statement to execute. Must include LIMIT.

plan_mode.name: kyuubi_query

plan_mode.description: List every file currently in this task's workspace, including files the user uploaded (`uploaded`), inputs received from previous tool calls (`input`), and previous-turn deliverables you wrote with write_file (`output`). Call this at the start of a turn whenever the user references 'the file we made', '上一轮的产物', '刚才那份报告', or any prior artifact you may have lost from context. Returns id/name/scope/format/size.
plan_mode.name: list_files

plan_mode.description: Read the contents of a file in this task's workspace. Accepts either the file's `id` (preferred — get from list_files) or its `name`. Text formats (.md/.txt/.csv/.tsv/.sql/.py/.json/.log/.yml/.yaml) return UTF-8 content; binary files return {is_binary: true} so you can advise the user to open it. Use this to pick up where a previous turn left off — e.g. read `query.sql` from last round before refining it.
plan_mode.name: read_file

plan_mode.description: Read a file from the current Agent's knowledge base (agents/<agent_id>/knowledge/<path>). Use this to fetch SQL templates, event-tracking indexes, page-structure specs, historical cases, and other reference data that is NOT part of the always-on system prompt. Start by reading `index.yaml` to see what's available, then drill in. Supports text formats (.yaml/.yml/.md/.json/.txt/.sql). Binary files (.db, images) are rejected — ask the user or use a different tool. Path must be relative within the agent's knowledge directory; '..' and absolute paths are rejected.

path.description: Relative path inside knowledge/, e.g. 'index.yaml', 'metrics/sql_templates/browser_feed.yaml', 'analysis/cases_and_lessons.yaml'.

plan_mode.name: read_agent_knowledge

plan_mode.description: Fetch the skill's instruction file. NOTE: this task has NO agentic skills bound — calling read_skill will return SKILL_NOT_FOUND for every skill_id. Ask the user to add a skill in the workspace 「🧰 本任务 Skills」 panel first, then retry.

path.description: Optional. Relative path inside the skill dir (e.g. 'reference/browser/table-schema.md'). 'ls' or '/' lists all files.

plan_mode.name: read_skill

plan_mode.description: Maintain a live TODO list for the current task so the user can see your progress. Use this when the task has 3+ distinct steps. Call with the FULL list every time (replace-all semantics, not diff-merge). Before you start working on an item, mark it in_progress. IMMEDIATELY after finishing, mark it completed — do NOT batch completions. At most ONE item may be in_progress at a time. If blocked, keep the item in_progress and add a new pending item describing the blocker.

activeForm.description: Present continuous form, e.g. 'Writing funnel SQL'

plan_mode.name: todo_write

plan_mode.description: Submit the completed plan to the user for approval and STOP generating. Call this ONLY when you have finished planning and have a concrete, actionable plan. After you call this tool, do NOT continue generating text — the user will review, and when they approve, you will be re-invoked with the green-light to execute. The plan argument should be a complete markdown description covering: what will be changed, in which files, and what verification to run.
plan_mode.name: exit_plan_mode

Pass the MCP tool name and its arguments as a JSON object. The server proxies the call to https://data-platform-mcp.mib.miui.com/mcp and returns the result as structured text. Use this whenever the user asks about 指标 / 维度 / 数据集 / 数据表 / 业务域 / 元数据 / 版本管理 / 指标一致性检测.

arguments.description: Arguments for the MCP tool. Each tool has its own schema; consult the skill docs for details.

tool_name.description: MCP tool name, e.g. 'metadata_config_list', 'metric_list', 'domain_tree', 'consistency_overview'. See the data-platform-mcp skill for the full list of 35 tools.

plan_mode.name: data_platform_call

subagent.description: Return the current UTC datetime in ISO 8601.
subagent.name: now

subagent.description: Echo back the given text. Useful for testing tool calling.
subagent.name: echo

subagent.description: Run a SELECT against Xiaomi Kyuubi SQL gateway. Read-only. The server already has the connection context configured (region=chnbj, workspace=11329, catalog=iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop, engine=presto, token=***). Do NOT ask the user for any of these — just call with the `sql` argument. Use fully-qualified table names like `iceberg_zjyprc_hadoop.<schema>.<table>`. Always include LIMIT.

sql.description: The SELECT statement to execute. Must include LIMIT.

subagent.name: kyuubi_query

subagent.description: Write text content to a file in the current task workspace. The file is registered under tasks/{task_id}/files/output/ and appears immediately in the user's left-side file panel. Use this whenever you produce a deliverable the user should be able to open / download / iterate on (markdown report, SQL script, CSV data, JSON, etc.). DO NOT use it for in-line answers.
subagent.name: write_file

subagent.description: Execute Python code in a sandboxed subprocess. Two main uses: (1) data analysis with pandas / numpy / scipy / sklearn / statsmodels / prophet / ruptures / matplotlib / seaborn pre-installed; (2) driving CLI tools that an agentic skill (feishu / kyuubi / datum / etc.) tells you to run — call them with subprocess.run([...], capture_output=True, text=True). Network is ON and the host's CLI auth files are reachable (HOME inherited), so `feishu fetch`, `kyuubi sql query`, `datum query` etc. work. Wall-clock + CPU timeout 60s, memory 1GB, output files captured under <task_workspace>/files/output/. cwd is files/output/. Print key results to stdout for the user; write artifacts to disk.

code.description: Full Python source. Use relative paths (cwd = <task_workspace>/files/output/). Print key results to stdout for the LLM/user to see; write artifacts to disk.

description.description: One-line summary of what this run computes (audit / display, not behavior).

timeout_sec.description: Optional override for wall-clock + CPU limit; defaults to ICE_PYTHON_SANDBOX_TIMEOUT_SEC.

subagent.name: execute_python

subagent.description: Run the volcano ABtest analysis pipeline for ONE experiment and return a markdown report. Wraps agents/volcano-abtest/scripts/analyze.py via the host's `datum` CLI — queries doris_zjyprc_hadoop ABtest tables (weighted aggregates + p-values + daily trends) and formats a report. Use this whenever the user asks for 火山实验 / abtest analysis with an experiment id + date range. Report is also saved to the task workspace as `abtest_<media>_<exp_id>_<start>-<end>.md` so it shows up in the user's file panel. Return field `report_md` is the same markdown — paste it into your reply, then append your own ### 实验分析 section.

end_date.description: End date. Same formats as start_date.

media.description: Media type. One of: 浏览器 / browser (浏览器), 内容中心 / 桌面内容中心 / newhome / nh / mcc (桌面内容中心).

subagent.name: volcano_abtest_analyze

subagent.description: Upload a PNG/JPG image from the task workspace to a Feishu doc and return its image_token (so subsequent feishu docx edits can embed it). Wraps `feishu docx upload-image <doc_token> --file <path>`. Use after `feishu_publish` creates the doc and `execute_python` produces a chart PNG under <task_workspace>/files/output/charts/.

image_path.description: Path relative to <task_workspace>/files/output/, e.g. 'charts/T3_forecast.png'. Absolute paths outside the task workspace are rejected.

subagent.name: feishu_upload_image

Use only for stable preferences, feedback, project context, or external reference pointers that should appear in future conversations. subagent.name: memory_save

subagent.description: Delete a stale or incorrect memory and remove it from MEMORY.md.
subagent.name: memory_delete

subagent.description: Overwrite the current task's STATE.md with a concise markdown summary of semantic progress: phase, decomposition, pending items, key decisions, current files, and next steps. Keep it under about 100 lines.
subagent.name: task_state_save

default.description: Publish a markdown document to Feishu (lark) via the bundled `feishu` CLI. By default lands in the team wiki space configured in FEISHU_DEFAULT_WIKI_SPACE_ID — every space member already has read access (no manual permission request needed). After creation, the task owner + active collaborators (those with a xiaomi_email on file) are auto-granted the FEISHU_AUTO_PERM_LEVEL permission; passing extra `share_to` emails grants them too. Returns the doc URL on success — INCLUDE the URL in your reply. 图表必须用 PNG（execute_python 出图 → feishu_upload_image 嵌入），不要在 markdown 里写 ```mermaid``` 块——飞书 PlantUML 渲染未开通，mermaid 会变成空白画板。返回值若带 content_warnings/hint 字段，说明文档里有渲染失败的块，按 hint 指引重试。

folder.description: Optional: put the doc under this drive folder token. Mutually exclusive with wiki_space / wiki_node.

share_perm.description: Permission level for share_to addresses. Defaults to 'edit'. Task owner + collaborators always get FEISHU_AUTO_PERM_LEVEL regardless.

share_to.description: Optional Xiaomi office emails (xxx@xiaomi.com / @mi.com) to grant explicit perms in addition to the task owner + collaborators. Use when sharing with a stakeholder who is not yet on the task.

wiki_node.description: Optional: put the doc under this wiki node (token or URL). Mutually exclusive with wiki_space / folder.

wiki_space.description: Optional override: place the doc under this wiki space root. Defaults to FEISHU_DEFAULT_WIKI_SPACE_ID.

To read a sibling reference file that the SKILL.md points you to (e.g. `reference/browser/table-schema.md`), pass `path` with the relative path. Pass `path="/"` or `path="ls"` to list all files in the skill folder. Content is read from the task's own snapshot, so answers are reproducible even if the global skill is updated later.

Two main uses: (1) data analysis with pandas / numpy / scipy / sklearn / statsmodels / prophet / ruptures / matplotlib / seaborn pre-installed; (2) driving CLI tools that an agentic skill (feishu / kyuubi / datum / etc.) tells you to run — call them with subprocess.run([...], capture_output=True, text=True). Network is ON and the host's CLI auth files are reachable (HOME inherited), so `feishu fetch`, `kyuubi sql query`, `datum query` etc. work. Wall-clock + CPU timeout 60s, memory 1GB, output files captured under <task_workspace>/files/output/. cwd is files/output/. Print key results to stdout for the user; write artifacts to disk.

Binary files (.db, images) are rejected — ask the user or use a different tool. Path must be relative within the agent's knowledge directory; '..' and absolute paths are rejected.

path.description: Relative path inside knowledge/, e.g. 'index.yaml', 'metrics/sql_templates/browser_feed.yaml', 'analysis/cases_and_lessons.yaml'.

subagent.name: read_agent_knowledge

subagent.description: Fetch the skill's instruction file. This task has these agentic skills bound: `kyuubi`, `feishu`, `nl-python`. Calling read_skill on any OTHER skill id returns SKILL_NOT_FOUND — only the listed ids are available. Default (no `path`) = returns SKILL.md. To read a sibling reference file that the SKILL.md points you to (e.g. `reference/browser/table-schema.md`), pass `path` with the relative path. Pass `path="/"` or `path="ls"` to list all files in the skill folder. Content is read from the task's own snapshot, so answers are reproducible even if the global skill is updated later.

path.description: Optional. Relative path inside the skill dir (e.g. 'reference/browser/table-schema.md'). 'ls' or '/' lists all files.

subagent.name: read_skill

subagent.description: Maintain a live TODO list for the current task so the user can see your progress. Use this when the task has 3+ distinct steps. Call with the FULL list every time (replace-all semantics, not diff-merge). Before you start working on an item, mark it in_progress. IMMEDIATELY after finishing, mark it completed — do NOT batch completions. At most ONE item may be in_progress at a time. If blocked, keep the item in_progress and add a new pending item describing the blocker.

activeForm.description: Present continuous form, e.g. 'Writing funnel SQL'

subagent.name: todo_write

subagent.description: Pause the current task and ask the user to provide a concrete decision, correction, approval, or missing business context. Use this only when continuing without a human answer would be unsafe, ambiguous, or likely to produce incorrect results. After calling this tool, stop and wait for the user's response.

fields.description: Optional input fields for the UI.

message.description: What you need from the user and why.

resume_prompt.description: Instruction to follow after the user resolves the request.

table.description: Optional rows needing review, e.g. {columns:[...], rows:[{...}]}

subagent.name: request_human_input
